risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A predetermined randomisation list was made by an independent statistician. Randomisation was performed in blocks to minimise the risk of revealing treatment group membership, with a variable size of three, six, nine or twelve participants." Comment: This describes a systematic random allocation process using an independent statistician and blocked randomisation with variable block sizes, which is consistent with a truly random sequence generation method.

Quote: "Randomisation was performed in blocks to minimise the risk of revealing treatment group membership, with a variable size of three, six, nine or twelve participants." and from the review characteristics: "Randomisation performed by an independent statistician who transferred the sequences to consecutively numbered envelopes." Comment: The review characteristics explicitly describe a concealment mechanism using consecutively numbered envelopes prepared by an independent statistician, which indicates recruiters could not foresee the next assignment before enrollment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The participants were older community-living individuals..." and "All three groups received written recommendations for fall prevention, which is standard care. In the OEP and MI groups, the OEP program was performed and the exercises were supervised on five occasions during the first three months." Comment: The intervention required active exercise training and motivational interviewing delivered by physiotherapists, so participants and personnel could not have been blinded to allocation. Because the main outcomes included subjective and participant-reported measures such as falls efficacy and falls, and the text indicates therapist interaction differed by group, knowledge of assignment could plausibly influence outcomes. Therefore the dominant risk for performance bias is high.

Quote: "All measurements were completed in the participant's home or at a health care centre formed by an independent PT who was blinded for group randomisation." Comment: Outcome assessment for the physical tests and reported falls was performed by an independent physiotherapist who was blinded to allocation, and the main effect outcomes included objective/standardized measures (Mini-BESTest, SPPB) plus monthly fall calendars. Although FES(S) was self-reported, the assessor for the non-self-report outcomes was blinded, and the study explicitly states blinded measurement for the independent PT, making detection bias unlikely for the assessed outcomes overall.

Quote: "The questionnaires for the feasibility procedure were analysed for all 45 participants for measurements and on 30 participants who were allocated to the treatment, OEP or MI groups... Three-month follow-ups were conducted for 40 participants." Comment: This indicates reasonably complete follow-up for the reported 3-month outcomes (40/45 overall retained; outcome analyses shown for 13, 14, and 13 participants per group in Table 3, consistent with only a few missing cases). The attrition appears modest and there is no report of differential dropout or reasons suggesting outcome-related missingness, so attrition is unlikely to have biased the results.
